description:
# Tighten the IR's safety contract

## Why

`Pattern::Tuple` lowering (fz-ben, src/ir_lower.rs:1949) emits a
TypeTest before any `Prim::TupleField` projection. The TypeTest is
correct (fz-ul4.36 made it actually check tuple shape at runtime).
The projection's emitted load is therefore guaranteed safe — but
the codegen still emits `notrap aligned`, declaring "this load
can't trap, don't generate trap metadata."

fz-ben's comment explicitly leaves this as a follow-up:
"could be tightened to `trap` later as a separate cleanup, but
not in scope here."

Now is later. The downstream chain (fz-ul4.36 schema-id TypeTest)
is in place; `notrap` was historically a safety net for the pre-
fz-ben unguarded projection, which no longer exists.

## What changes

Tighten `MemFlags::trusted()` (which is aligned + notrap) to
`MemFlags::new().with_aligned()` (aligned + trap) on:

- The `load` in `Prim::TupleField` codegen
  (src/ir_codegen.rs:5505-5509).
- Any other load emitted for a TypeTest-gated projection.

Honest behavior: if the IR ever emits a bad TupleField (it
shouldn't post-fz-ben), the program SIGSEGVs at the bad load
instead of reading heap garbage and continuing with corrupt
values.

## What stays

- Every other `MemFlags::trusted()` site (closures, headers,
  bitstrings, etc.). Each was verified safe by its own ticket;
  this ticket only touches tuple-field loads.
- `aligned` — the loads ARE aligned by construction.

## What's removed

- The implicit "silently corrupt on bad pattern" failure mode
  for tuple field projection.
- A textually stale flag.
- The fz-ben TODO note.

## Atomic steps

1. Identify every `load` emitted for Pattern::Tuple's
   TupleField projection (one site today; verify).
2. Change `MemFlags` accordingly.
3. Re-bless every fixture golden CLIF — one `notrap aligned` →
   `trap aligned` flag flip per tuple-field load. Most
   fixtures will have zero changes (no tuple patterns).
   ast_eval will have one flag change per field projection.
4. Update fz-ben's now-obsolete comment.

## Acceptance

- All fixture matrix trials pass.
- ast_eval and any other tuple-pattern fixture's CLIF flips
  `notrap aligned` → `trap aligned` on the relevant loads.
- No runtime behavior change for correct programs.

## Sequence

Land **last** in this arc (after fz-ul4.40/.41/.42/.43) so the
golden re-bless happens once on a stable spec/dispatch shape,
not against intermediate states.

## Risk

`notrap` may serve as a Cranelift speculation hint; switching to
`trap` may marginally restrict instruction reordering. Negligible
for our workload; verify no perf regression on the bitstring
microbench if we have one.
title: codegen: notrap → trap on TypeTest-gated tuple field loads
